description_long: This house is a large, intricately designed wooden structure with multiple octagonal towers topped by onion domes and Orthodox crosses, reminiscent of traditional Russian or Eastern European ecclesiastical architecture. The exterior is clad in dark wooden planks, with a prominent covered entryway extending from the main building, supported by wooden posts and featuring a steeply pitched roof. The rooflines are complex, with several tiers and overhanging eaves, and the main body of the house is flanked by smaller annexes and porches. Windows are small and sparse, mostly rectangular, and are set symmetrically on the main facades. The structure sits elevated above the ground, with visible support beams and a wraparound porch area. Decorative wooden trim and detailed roofwork add to the ornate appearance of the building.
description: A large, ornate wooden house with multiple onion domes, steeply pitched roofs, and a prominent covered entryway, inspired by traditional Russian architecture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main covered entryway, central tower with onion dome, and symmetrical side extensions.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the layered roof structure, side annex, and the extended covered porch area.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the symmetrical back facade, two main towers with domes, and minimal window openings.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view showing the complex rooflines, side porch, and the building's elevated base.
attribute_roof_type: onion dome with gable and multi-tiered pitched roofs
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with small rectangular windows
attribute_entryway_design: Large covered entryway with steeply pitched roof, wooden posts, and decorative trim
